summary: You are given a list of meeting time intervals, each written as [start, end]. Return the smallest number of rooms required so that every meeting can take place, given that overlapping meetings must each occupy their own room.

examples.explanation: [0, 30] runs the whole time, while [5, 10] and [15, 20] fit one after another in a second room.
The two meetings never overlap, so a single room is enough.

constraints: Each interval is [start, end] with start <= end
An empty list of meetings requires 0 rooms
A meeting that starts exactly when another ends can reuse the same room
This is a LeetCode Premium problem
